description: Individual farewell monologues after the final victory. Each character reflects on their journey.

epilogues.robin_hood: Sherwood is calling. I can smell the oak trees and hear the river. But out here... out here I'm more than just an outlaw. I'm a LEADER. And leaders don't run home when the fight is over. They stay. They build.
epilogues.alice: Wonderland was always mad. But this adventure... THIS was the maddest tea party of all. And the best. I went down the rabbit hole a curious girl. I came out a woman who knows that curiosity without empathy is just selfishness.
epilogues.scrooge: I've been given three chances at redemption already. The ghosts of Christmas Past, Present, and Future. This? This was the FOURTH chance. And this time, I didn't need ghosts to show me the way. I just needed family.
epilogues.peter_pan: If I go back to Neverland, I'll never grow up. But staying here... I've already grown more than I thought possible. Someone once told me growing up means losing the magic. But standing here, with all of you... I've never felt more magical.
epilogues.wicked_witch: Oz never wanted me. They cheered when I melted. But HERE — here I was needed. Here, being different wasn't a curse. It was a GIFT. And I will never apologize for being wicked again.
epilogues.phantom: The song I've sung here — fighting alongside friends who never saw my face and STILL stood beside me — that is my masterpiece. Not the music of the night. The music of BELONGING.
epilogues.sherlock: I've solved every case London ever offered. But this mystery — why strangers become family — that one I'll never fully deduce. And for once, I'm perfectly content with not knowing.
epilogues.tarzan: Jungle is... home. Always home. But tribe is here now. Home is... the people who fight beside you. Tarzan learned many words in books. But 'family' — that word, Tarzan learned HERE.
epilogues.dracula: Fighting alongside the living — feeling the warmth of their courage — I would trade eternity for one more day of this. There was a time I thought warmth was something I'd never feel again. I was wrong.
epilogues.merlin: I saw this moment eight hundred years ago. Every one of you, standing together. I saw it in the stars, in the stones, in the crystal cave. But seeing it and FEELING it are very different things. This is better than any prophecy.
epilogues.frankenstein: Go back? Back to... the villagers with torches? The ice? The loneliness? No. Here I am... not monster. Here I am... family. That word. Family. Frankenstein's favorite word.
epilogues.shadow_author: I spent eight hundred years collecting characters because I had none of my own. Now I have seventeen. Seventeen souls who forgave the unforgivable. I will spend the rest of eternity being worthy of that forgiveness.
epilogues.captain_hook: I fought Peter Pan for a century. A CENTURY of swords and shadows and that infernal ticking. But fighting BESIDE him? That was the greatest adventure of all. Don't tell him I said that.
epilogues.queen_of_hearts: I sentenced a thousand souls. 'Off with their heads!' I screamed it until my throat was raw. But here, I learned a better sentence. 'You are pardoned.' It feels... surprisingly good.
epilogues.clayton: I was rewritten as a villain. The Author made me hunt what I once protected. But the heroes saw past the rewrite. They saw the conservationist underneath. I will spend the rest of my story proving them right.
epilogues.headless_horseman: The Horseman rides no more. The soldier... has come home. I have a name now. I have a purpose. And I have something the hollow never gave me — peace.
epilogues.medusa: For three thousand years, everything I touched turned to stone. But today, someone held my hand. Actually HELD it. And nothing happened. Except I cried. That was new.
epilogues.anubis: The scales are balanced. The story is complete. And it is... good. For five thousand years I weighed hearts and found them wanting. Today, I weighed my own. The feather smiled.
